calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-08-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
William D. Rauch for Marilyn Saner, Petitioner/Executor (via Zoom).

Discussion was held regarding the court's tentative ruling. The court noted that accounting costs are typically considered an ordinary service under local rules and would likely need to be deducted from ordinary service fees. The court indicated it intended to approve the request for extraordinary fees.

Mr. Rauch requested the court consider charging the cost of accounting to the estate, citing the relatively small value of the estate and significant work required due to the decedent's unpaid taxes. The court agreed to review this request further and took the matter under submission with the intent to grant.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-15-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

This matter was put on for hearing with no appearance required for the purpose of admitting the will. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated April 15, 2019 is admitted to probate. Marilyn Saner is appointed as the Executor of the estate with full authority. Bond is not required. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for October 10, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status on administration is filed two weeks prior to the hearing date, the status hearing will go off calendar. Frank Mahoney III is appointed Probate Referee.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Wong. Not reported.
